data.title: Microsoft refutes TD Cowen report it is canceling US data center leases (update)
data.description:
Update: Microsoft issued a statement to Jefferies obtained by CNBC that refuted the TD Cowen note. "The broker report may also have some misunderstanding about Microsoft's definition of leasing," the statement said. "It includes deals over 15 years in length where the underlying owner of the [data center] servers is not Microsoft but they operate it. Microsoft's use of third party REITs is in fact quite low."

Despite Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stating last month that it would spend $80B on data centers this year, with more than half of that in the U.S., investment firm TD Cowen said the Satya Nadella-led company may be canceling some leases.

Shares erased premarket gains and fell nearly 2% in early trading.

"Our channel checks indicate that [Microsoft] has cancelled leases in the U.S. totaling a 'couple hundred' [megawatts] with at least two private data center operators," analysts led by Michael Elias wrote in a note on Friday. The analysts also noted that Microsoft has "pulled back on the conversion of [statement of qualifications] to leases" and has re-allocated a "considerable portion" of its international spend to the U.S.

"When coupled with our prior channel checks, it points to a potential oversupply position for Microsoft," the analysts added.

It is currently unclear if there is simply a delay in the transition from statement of qualifications into a signed lease, or if it's an outright termination, the analysts added. They also said they have not gotten "the level of color" via their channel checks on why this is occurring. They suggested the pullback could be tied to its deal with OpenAI, which may be working more closely with Japanese conglomerate SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) in the future.

"However, as we believe is indicated by its decision to pause construction on a data center in Wisconsin which our prior channel checks indicated was to support OpenAI, there is capacity that it has likely procured, particularly in areas where capacity is not fungible to cloud, where the company may have excess data center capacity relative to its new forecast," the analysts posited.

They also stressed that this is their interpretation of the current situation, and it may change with added channel checks.

Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Hasbro inks deal to distribute the iconic Stretch Armstrong toy line
data.description:
Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) and Basic Fun! announced a worldwide licensing agreement on Monday.

Basic Fun! was described as a dynamic global designer and marketer of classic, innovative children's entertainment products which strive to bring smiles to people of all ages. The company has renowned iconic brands and a broad product portfolio that are featured in over 50,000 brick and mortar locations and online retail distribution points by more than 2,500 leading retailers, distributors, and family entertainment venues in over 60 countries worldwide.

The global licensing agreement will cover Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) manufacturing and distributing classic and reimagined products under the iconic Stretch Armstrong toy brand that originated the first super-stretchy action figure characters almost 50 years ago. Basic Fun!'s first Stretch Armstrong toys are expected to arrive at retailers worldwide starting in fall 2025.

Stretch Armstrong was introduced by Kenner Products in 1976. The original concept was created by Jesse Horowitz, an industrial designer for Kenner's R&D group, and developed with James "Jeep" Kuhn, the company's vice president of R&D36. Initially, two designs were considered: a sumo wrestler and an All-American blond figure. The latter was chosen and became the iconic Stretch Armstrong

"At Hasbro, we're always looking for ways to bring our brands to life for new generations of fans," stated Hasbro senior director Bradley Bowman. "Stretch Armstrong has always been a symbol of fun, imagination and endless possibilities," he added.

Other toy franchises that Hasbro (HAS) has licensing agreements include Marvel, Star Wars, Transformers, Power Rangers, Ghostbusters, Barbie, and Jurassic Park/Jurassic World.

data.title: Dollar moves up slightly after last week’s declines - Currency Recap
data.description:
The U.S. dollar was slightly up on Monday after suffering a sustained sell-off from over the last week.

The greenback was up 0.12% at $106.74 on Monday, after seeing a setback last week from soft economic data, and disappointing sales forecast from retail giant Walmart (WMT), which is viewed as a bellwether of U.S. consumer spending.

Dollar weekly moves and key drivers:

The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the dollar against a basket of six foreign currencies, fell 0.35% over the last week.

The dollar’s fall last week was triggered by multiple factors, such as soft economic data. Data from S&P Global on Friday showed U.S. business activity dropped to a 17-month low this month.

The greenback fell further after declines seen in the University of Michigan sentiment report and U.S. existing home sales data.The reports kept the prospect of interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve intact this year.

Some of the pullback in the dollar last week was also due to a softer-than-anticipated sales forecast from Walmart (WMT) sparking concerns over the health of the American consumer.

Additionally, investors grew concerned regarding Trump’s plans for tariffs, which are being increasingly seen as potential negotiating ploys rather than policy objectives. Trump also said last week that a potential trade deal with China was possible.

The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 7 basis points over the week to 4.45%, while the shorter-end, 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 points to 4.23%.

Currency Highlights: Major currency movements (Feb 17 to Feb 23)

Euro (EUR:USD)

+0.55%

Pound Sterling (GBP:USD)

+0.64%

Japanese Yen (JPY:USD)

+2.13%

Chinese Yuan (CNY:USD)

+0.50%

Swiss Franc (CHF:USD)

+0.67%

Australian Dollar (AUD:USD)

+0.04%

Canadian Dollar (CAD:USD)

+0.05%

The Euro (EUR:USD) was last up 0.08% against the dollar continuing its gains from last week. Euro’s rise on Monday was in part due to the victory of Germany's conservatives in the national election.

Friedrich Merz's win to become the next Chancellor of Germany led the euro to see an initial surge to its highest level in over two weeks when compared to the pound.

The Pound Sterling (GBP:USD) also ticked up against the dollar on Monday, extending its gains from last week. Pound reached a two-month high on Friday after UK’s consumer spending saw an unexpected sharp rise.

The Japanese yen was down 0.45% against the greenback on Monday, retreating from its last week’s rally. Yen’s rally last week was driven by a sell-off in Japanese government bonds after the national core inflation hit a 19-month peak in January, fuelling expectations of more rate hikes in Japan.

However, BOJ cheif Kazuo Ueda quickly crushed hopes of a rate hike, saying the central bank could contain long-term interest rates by buying government bonds.

Market reactions and analysis:

According to Bank of America's global research team, there has lately been a significant shift in market sentiment in its latest fund manager survey, noting that the primary tail risk now worrying investors is the escalating trade wars initiated by Trump's tariff moves.

As per the BofA survey, 42% of respondents now see global trade war as the most bearish development for risk assets in 2025, up from January's 30%. Fund managers see "the disorderly" rise in bond yields and federal rate hikes as the second and third-most bearish developments.

Speaking on global trade concerns, Karl Schamotta, chief market strategist, at Corpay in Toronto, said, "The greenback is undergoing a technical rebound after suffering a sustained selloff in recent weeks, and other currencies are also seeing risk discounts come back as trade worries return."

Economic Indicators to watch:

Inflation data will take the prime spot this week, with the scheduled release of Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index for January on Friday.

Other important economic indicators set to be released this week include the consumer confidence survey on Tuesday, and some housing data like pricing data on Tuesday, new home sales results on Wednesday, and pending home sales on Thursday.

Thursday will also see the revisions to fourth-quarter GDP. Several central bankers are also expected to deliver remarks this week, including Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan, and Philadelphia Fed President Patrick Harker.

Dollar-Based ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN).

data.title: SharpLink Gaming plunges following 10% acquisition of Armchair Enterprises
data.description:
• SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET) has acquired a 10% equity stake in U.K.-based Armchair Enterprises which owns and operates CryptoCasino.com, the company said on Monday.
  • The acquisition was made for $500,000 in cash, along with a right of first refusal to acquire a controlling interest in Armchair.
  • SBET is -10.3% to $0.3999.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: A.I.S. Resources announces private placement financing, debt transaction and CEO transition
data.description:
• A.I.S. Resources (AIS:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 2.86M units at a price of $0.035/unit for gross proceeds of $100,000.
  • The proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • Each private placement unit consists of one common share and one transferable share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one additional common share for a period of 2 years from the closing date of the offering at $0.05/share.
  • AIS has also entered into debt settlement agreements with various creditors to settle an aggregate amount of $625,444 in outstanding debt related to services provided to AIS through the issuance of an aggregate of 12.51M shares at $0.05/share.
  • AIS announced the appointment of Marc Enright-Morin as president and chief executive of the company.
  • Andrew Neale resigned as CEO and director.
  • Morin has served as CEO, president, and director of multiple publicly traded companies in the resource sector.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: OSI Systems receives $32M security order
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) announced that its security division received an international order valued at ~$32M to provide radio frequency based critical systems that are designed for long-range secure communications.
  • The company is expected to support the systems integration and deployment activities for this program.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: QCR announces CEO retirement, CFO Todd Gipple appointed as successor
data.description:
• Multi-bank holding company QCR Holdings (NASDAQ:QCRH) announced on Monday the retirement of Larry Helling from the CEO post.
  • Helling is also set to retire from the board.
  • President and CFO Todd Gipple has been appointed the president and CEO.
  • Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer Nick Anderson is set to become the CFO.
  • The retirement and the consequent appointments are effective immediately following the May 22 stockholders meeting.
  • QCRH shares were trading -0.48% pre-market at $76.21.

data.title: Tesla prepares to deploy full self driving features in China
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is preparing a software update for its customers in China, offering driver-assistance features similar to the full self-driving capabilities available in the US, according to a Bloomberg report.

The update will enable Tesla owners to access driver-assist features on city streets, such as guiding vehicles to exit ramps and intersections, recognizing traffic signals, and managing lane changes and speed adjustments.

As per the report, these capabilities will be available to customers who have paid 64,000 yuan for FSD. The feature will initially roll out on select models in the coming days, with plans for gradual expansion.

Chief executive Elon Musk has been seeking regulatory approval for FSD to operate on Chinese roads for a few years. The company has been facing challenges with FSD in China due to restrictions from both Beijing and Washington on how the system is trained to navigate local roads.

To overcome this, Tesla engineers were using publicly available videos of Chinese streets from the internet to train the system. Musk aims to launch the FSD system in China this year.

TSLA shares were up 1.8% premarket on Monday.

data.title: CarGurus CFO Elisa Palazzo to step down
data.description:
• CarGurus (NASDAQ:CARG) announced Monday that effective March 7, Elisa Palazzo will step down as the company’s chief financial officer.
  • Ms. Palazzo was appointed the company's CFO in October 2023.
  • CEO Jason Trevisan will serve as the company’s principal financial and accounting officer beginning March 7 until Ms. Palazzo’s successor is named.
  • Shares opened 2% lower, below the 200-day moving average for the first time since August.

data.title: Smithfield Foods gains after seeing mostly bull ratings after its post-IPO quiet period expires
data.description:
Smithfield Foods (NASDAQ:SFD) opened on Monday with a swing higher after the post-IPO quiet period on the stock expired on Monday to free up analysts to post ratings.

The food processing stock landed a majority of bull ratings, including from Morgan Stanley (Overweight, price target $28), Barclays (Overweight, PT $28), Citi (Buy, PT $27), UBS (Buy, PT $26), Bank of America (Buy, PT $28), and Goldman Sachs (Buy, PT $32).

BofA's Buy rating on Smithfield Foods (NASDAQ:SFD) was based on its view that the company has a superior financial profile versus its closest peers, supported by its solid execution and favorablemacroeconomic trends. "SFD has been able to expand margins, gain share, and innovate in a difficult category," highlighted analyst Peter Galbo. It was also highlighted that high-protein diets are on trend for U.S. consumers and that the pork cycle is currently advantaged versus beef and chicken.

Meanwhile, Exane BNP Paribas came in more cautious on Smithfield Foods (SFD), with a Neutral rating and price target of $23.

Shares of Smithfield Foods (SFD) were up 1.9% in early trading on Monday to $21.50 vs. the post-IPO range of $19.10 to $22.03. The IPO was priced at $20 per share.
